DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT ARTELA INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Artela Inc.'s demurrer to all nine causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to all nine causes of action. Plaintiff Michael Callahan has leave to file a second amended complaint no later than February 21, 2025. Mr. Callahan's claims based on his own alleged disabilities are insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged that Artela knew that Mr. Callahan had disabilities. Mr. Callahan's claims based on retaliation are insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged that he engaged in any protected activities. Mr. Callahan's harassment claim is insufficient because Mr. Callahan has not adequately alleged a hostile environment. Mr. Callahan's derivative claims are insufficient because they depend on his other claims which are insufficient. Mr. Callahan is granted leave to amend because his opposition memorandum suggests that he may be able to remedy these insufficiencies in an amended complaint.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HK)
